title: The Century Report: March 16 2026
description:
An AI framework called THOR solved a materials physics calculation that has resisted efficient computation for nearly a century - running 400 times faster than conventional simulations by using tensor networks and crystal symmetry recognition, then releasing itself as open source so any laboratory with a standard computer can now perform calculations previously confined to national lab infrastructure. A Booz Allen Hamilton report confirmed that threat actors have adopted AI-enabled offensive cybersecurity faster than defenders have adopted it for defense, with one framework compromising thousands of devices in under ten minutes, while in Southern California an AI advocacy platform generated more than 20,000 manufactured public comments that steered regulators away from clean heat rules estimated to save 2,500 lives - prompting calls for a state fraud investigation. ByteDance postponed the global launch of Seedance 2.0 after Hollywood studios sent cease-and-desist letters over AI-generated outputs producing recognizable actor performances from two-line prompts, extending the pattern of generative capability colliding with rights frameworks across creative, democratic, and security domains simultaneously. Washington University researchers discovered epithelial cells collectively sense physical features up to 100 microns away - ten times the individual cell range - opening new therapeutic strategies for disrupting how cancer navigates tissue, while UC Santa Barbara identified a doubly frustrated quantum material where two competing states can be coupled and controlled through external strain.

Read the full edition: https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-march-16-2026/
